# BI-260004: Apply implementation boundaries for archive reuse and launch scope

- Backlog Item ID: BI-260004
- Title: Enforce section-6 implementation boundaries in public-site build
- Persona/Actor: Public-site maintainer and visitor
- Value statement: For maintainers and visitors, deliver a clean and reliable public site by
  enforcing visual/content reuse boundaries and minimal launch integrations.
- Context: Section 6 decisions are accepted and must now guide all implementation work.
- Problem statement: Without explicit boundary enforcement, implementation may reintroduce archive
  patterns, stale copy, and unnecessary launch dependencies.
- Expected outcome: Public-site implementation follows approved keep/rewrite/drop rules with clear
  launch scope limits.
- Success metric(s): Boundary violations are absent from release candidates; legacy high-value links
  remain functional through compatibility routing.
- Acceptance criteria (behavior):
  - Approved asset categories are reused; disallowed layout systems are not.
  - Marketing copy is rewritten and legal/support content is reviewed.
  - No analytics/form backend integration is present at launch unless approved.
  - Contact flow uses contact page plus mailto fallback.
  - Compatibility routes/pages exist for important legacy URLs.
- Non-goals: Building a full redirect infrastructure outside static hosting constraints.
- Constraints: Static hosting on GitHub Pages, no server runtime on the public site.
- Dependencies: Final copy review and legacy URL inventory.
- Risks/assumptions: Some legacy links may be unknown until traffic or user feedback reveals them.
- Validation approach: Manual route checks plus content-review checklist during release review.
- Priority score: High
- Target window: Before first public production release
- Links:
  - Requirement entry: REQ-FR-260004.md
  - ADR: ADR-260004.md
  - ICR: n/a
  - PR/changeset: n/a
